name: Stirling Castle, Loch Lomond & The Trossachs from Edinburgh
tagline: Scotland's most important castle and its most beautiful loch in a single Highland day
overview: Stirling Castle was where Scottish kings were crowned, Mary Queen of Scots was raised, and the fate of the nation was decided. Combine it with the sparkling shores of Loch Lomond — Scotland's largest loch and first National Park — for a day that covers Highland history and Highland scenery.
highlights: Guided tour of Stirling Castle, Scotland's most important royal fortress
Scenic drive through The Trossachs National Park
Stop at Loch Lomond, Scotland's largest loch
Learn the stories of William Wallace and Mary Queen of Scots

itinerary.description: Explore the great hall, royal chapel, and the Palace of James V. Hear the stories of William Wallace, Robert the Bruce, and the young Mary Queen of Scots, who was crowned here aged nine months.
Free time on the shores of Scotland's largest and most beautiful loch. Optional boat cruise available. Walk the village of Luss, one of Scotland's prettiest.

meeting_point: Edinburgh city centre — confirmed at booking
tips: Stirling Castle is at altitude — bring a windproof layer
Combine with the Loch Ness tour over two days for the ultimate Scottish Highland experience
The Stirling Castle great hall is one of the finest examples of medieval Scottish architecture

faqs.question: Is Stirling Castle admission included?
faqs.answer: Yes, Stirling Castle entry is included in the tour price.

faqs.question: How does this differ from the Loch Ness tour?
faqs.answer: This tour focuses on Stirling and the southern Highlands (Loch Lomond). The Loch Ness tour heads north to Eilean Donan and the northern Highlands. Both are excellent and complement each other perfectly.

hero_image.alt: Stirling Castle perched on volcanic rock above the Scottish lowlands

meta.title: Stirling Castle & Loch Lomond Day Trip from Edinburgh
meta.description: Visit Stirling Castle and Loch Lomond on a guided day trip from Edinburgh. Admission included. From £39 per person.
